content.full_text:
ROSSMOOR COMMUNITY SERVICES DISTRICT
3232 HEDWIG ROAD, ROSSMOOR, CA 90720 / (213) 430-3707

Nov 6 9 3a.m '90

Novemeber 1, 1990


Mr. Wayne Imberi
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

Dear Mr. Imberi:

This letter follows our phone conversation on September 27,
1990.  I have enclosed a copy of the questions I had asked
and your responses.  Please send a written response confirm-
ing your responses for our records as soon as possible.

Thank you for your cooperation in this matter.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Yvette Cartee
Administrative Assistant

Enc.

Phone conversation with the California Fair Political Practices Commission,
September 27, 1990, 9:15 am.

Wayne Imberi, Consultant

Question #1

An incumbent, who is not running for re-election, because she is moving
away from the area, would like to write a letter to the community, to be
handed out as a flyer and/or printed in the newspapers. The letter would
be a a type of goodbye and thank you of sorts and possibly express support
for two or more candidates running in the upcoming election. The candidates
have not solicited her support.

Answer:

The consultant sees no problem. There would be no limit on the independent
expenditure, however; if more than $1,000.00 is expended, she must file a
statement of independent expenditure.


Question #2

Can a current Board Member, who is not up for re-election at this time, send
out a flyer, to be handed out and inserted into the newspapers, that shows the
voting record of the five Board Members on ten to twelve issues.

Answer:

If the flyer is to be handed out, the sender has no obligation to identify
themselves.

If the flyer is to be inserted into newspapers which are sent through the
mail, and you are sending over 200 pieces, the sender has no obligation
to identify themselves, unless it is apparent from the context of the
flyer that the sender is specifically advocating the defeat or election
of any candidate, then the sender must be identified by name and address.
